page.content: The data presented in Table 1 offers critical insights into the association between exposure to specific Parkinson-disease proteins and various health conditions, particularly focusing on cardiac health and respiratory symptoms. With a notable emphasis on the adjusted odds ratios (OR) and their corresponding 95% confidence intervals (CI), the table elucidates the differential impact of protein exposure on participants diagnosed with cardiac conditions.

lines.text: The data presented in Table 1 offers critical insights into the association between exposure to specific Parkinson-disease
proteins and various health conditions, particularly focusing on cardiac health and respiratory symptoms. With a notable
emphasis on the adjusted odds ratios (OR) and their corresponding 95% confidence intervals (CI), the table elucidates the
differential impact of protein exposure on participants diagnosed with cardiac conditions.

cells.cell_text: Acute ischemia/reperfusion injuryHeart failure, cardiac development
Acute ischemia/reperfusion injuryLeft ventricular hypertrophy, heart failure, diabetic heart

page.content: Specifically, the crude OR for acute ischemia/reperfusion injury reveals a significant correlation, highlighting the potential implications of Parkinson-related proteins on cardiac health. The mean PAI (N) score of 36.42 (SD = 35.82) further underscores the variability in exposure levels among the study participants. Notably, the data indicates that individuals with exposure (n = 31) exhibited a marked increase in respiratory symptoms compared to those with no exposure (n = 31), with reported percentages of 29.0% versus 3.3%, respectively. This information is crucial for understanding the broader health implications of Parkinson-disease proteins and emphasizes the need for further research in this area.
